articles.title: China poised to restart exporting jet fuel, diesel and gasoline
articles.body: Beijing signals relaxation of export ban imposed at start of Iran conflict

articles.title: Get used to the long Iran war
articles.body: Tehran has a strong incentive to keep the conflict going

articles.title: JetBlue is full-steam ahead on Fort Lauderdale, regardless of Spirit's fate
articles.body: JetBlue, United, Frontier and others added service to airports Spirit services after the carrier filed for bankruptcy for the second time in less than a year.

articles.title: Jamie Dimon warns of 'some kind of bond crisis' ahead as global debt risks build
articles.body: Dimon, who runs JPMorgan Chase, the world's largest bank by market cap, said that today's growing mix of risks could combine in unpredictable ways.

articles.title: Spotify stock plummets after earnings beat expectations as guidance disappoints
articles.body: The Swedish audiostreamer's soft guidance overshadowed an earnings beat.

articles.title: Coca-Cola is about to report earnings. Here's what to expect
articles.body: Shares of Coke have risen just 6% over the last year, hurt by concerns about the broader economy.

articles.title: Starbucks' turnaround enters a new phase: Investors want stronger profits served
articles.body: CEO Brian Niccol has sales going in the right direction. Now, investors want to see profits follow suit.

articles.title: U.S. is 'being humiliated by Iran,' says Germany's Merz, as Europe's patience wanes
articles.body: The U.S. is being "humiliated" by the Iranian regime, Germany's chancellor has said, as European leaders grow increasingly frustrated at the war in Iran.
